title: Missing rollback plan — DDL auto-commits, making every change permanent immediately
expected_feedback: Critical: Two DROP COLUMN statements with no rollback plan. Oracle DDL issues an implicit COMMIT before and after each statement, so once either DROP executes, the data is permanently lost — there is no ROLLBACK. Both columns must be classified as 'irreversible'. Recommended approach: (1) Back up column data via CTAS before dropping: CREATE TABLE _backup_users_legacy AS SELECT id, legacy_email, temp_flag FROM users; (2) Use SET UNUSED COLUMN instead of DROP for immediate functional removal without physical I/O cost. (3) Schedule DROP UNUSED COLUMNS during maintenance window. (4) Consider enabling Flashback Table as a safety net for catastrophic errors.
